<!-- Topic 1: What's the Output? -->
<!-- Total slides: 4 -->

# What's the Output?

---

## new, delete, and what the pointer holds after {.smaller}

+ Two exercises on heap allocation mechanics.
+ Work each one out before the answer is revealed.

::: notes
Total slides: 4
Give students 60–90 seconds per exercise. These are warm-up — the point is to surface assumptions about what delete does to the pointer.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## Exercise 1 — Code {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    int* p = new int(100);
    int* q = p;

    cout << *p << "\n";   // line A

    delete p;
    p = nullptr;

    cout << (p == nullptr ? "null" : "not null") << "\n";  // line B
    cout << (q == nullptr ? "null" : "not null") << "\n";  // line C

    return 0;
}
```

::: notes
Line A: 100 — p points to a heap int initialized to 100.
Line B: null — p was explicitly set to nullptr after delete.
Line C: not null — q still holds the original address; setting p = nullptr did not affect q. q is now a dangling pointer.
Key point: delete frees the memory but does not touch the pointer variable. Only the assignment p = nullptr does that — and it only touches p.
:::

<!-- Slide 2 -->

---

## Exercise 2 — Code {.smaller}

```{.cpp}
#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

int main() {
    int n = 3;
    int* arr = new int[n];

    arr[0] = 10;
    arr[1] = 20;
    arr[2] = 30;

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        cout << arr[i] << " ";   // line A
    cout << "\n";

    delete[] arr;
    arr = nullptr;

    cout << n << "\n";           // line B

    return 0;
}
```

::: notes
Line A: 10 20 30 — standard subscript access on a heap array works identically to a stack array.
Line B: 3 — n is a stack variable; delete[] arr has no effect on it whatsoever.
Key point: delete[] frees the heap block; nothing else in the program changes.
:::

<!-- Slide 3 -->

---

## What these exercises reveal

- `delete` frees the memory — it does not zero the pointer
- Setting `p = nullptr` only affects `p` — any other pointer to the same block is now dangling
- Stack variables are completely unaffected by heap operations

<!-- Slide 4 -->
